mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, mechanically depressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rate-hiking periods are not significantly lower than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals a rapid labor market deterioration, triggering immediate equity sell-offs as investors price in recession risks over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >55% of instances when UNRATE spikes >0.2pp month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: The joint divergence of declining real savings funds flows and accelerating consumer price inflation creates a dual shock to household purchasing power and liquidity, driving negative S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon as consumption demand collapses.
failure_condition: The interaction term yields a statistically insignificant coefficient (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression of 60-day forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: An upward revision in the index of industrial production relative to its prior vintage indicates stronger-than-expected manufacturing output, which generates positive short-term S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as markets reprice corporate revenue expectations upward.
failure_condition: Vintage revisions to INDPRO show no predictive power for 5-day forward returns (Rank IC < 0.01) across all post-shock recovery periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index surpasses the 295.0 threshold, it confirms entrenched inflationary pressures that force central banks to maintain restrictive policy, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in >60% of observations where CPIAUCSL > 295.0.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising corporate revenues, which mechanically supports equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year Rank IC for PAYEMS growth falls below 0.02, or forward returns are negative in >50% of months with payroll growth.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous expansion in housing starts and real GDP growth signals robust aggregate demand and construction sector activity, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 60 days as cyclical equities benefit from higher capital expenditure and consumer spending.
failure_condition: The joint effect of HOUST and GDPC1 growth yields a negative or insignificant coefficient in predicting 60-day forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: A concurrent decline in industrial production and rise in unemployment during a tightening cycle indicates a supply-side contraction and weakening labor demand, which triggers negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days as recession probabilities increase.
failure_condition: The interaction signal fails to predict negative returns when both series move in the specified directions, with a hit rate below 40%.
